title: atomic-copy / UI copy contract
description: data/copy.json — the site's user-facing UI chrome as copy atoms: the verbal counterpart to the brand design tokens (see docs/atomic-copy.md). Each property (other than _source) is a copy atom: a stable dotted id (e.g. corpus.eyebrow, work.eyebrow, colophon.eyebrow, proof.prefix, resume.section.skills) mapped to its exact rendered string. build.mjs resolves them via copy(id), which throws on an unknown id, so a template can't reference an atom that doesn't exist — the verbal analogue of an undefined CSS custom property. Holds only static chrome (eyebrows, section headings, figure labels, fixed prose connectors, button labels) — copy that derives from the content contracts (profile.json / presentation.json / site.json) is NOT duplicated here.

additionalProperties.description: A copy atom — the exact, non-empty rendered string for its dotted id.

_source.description: Provenance note for human editors. Not a copy atom.
